reason: Despite positive Wall Street news, Bitcoin failed to sustain gains above $70,000, with rapid profit-taking evident at $74,000. Macroeconomic concerns, highlighted by issues with a BlackRock fund impacting crypto and DeFi, coupled with persistent regulatory scrutiny (Binance probe), indicate a market in a distribution phase with significant downside pressure. Weakness in the broader financial system is compounding selling pressure, making accumulation risky at current levels without clearer support validation.
headlines: Why bitcoin couldn't hold $70,000 despite its best week of Wall Street news in months
Managing financial AI agents is the only skill you’ll need to survive the AI layoffs
BlackRock private credit fund is latest to crack, hitting crypto prices and DeFi markets
Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
